original: Japanese researchers from University of Yamanashi report birth of mice from freeze-dried sperm stored on the International Space Station
Video game Pac-Man created by Toru Iwatani is first released in Japan
Shuji Nakamura, Japanese-born American electronic engineer (invented the Blue LED), born in Ikata, Japan
Wright Brothers are granted a patent for their "flying machine," having applied for one 3 years earlier (patent no. 821,393)
1-armed pitcher Hugh Daily fanned 13 hitters
Stafford & Cernan pilot Apollo 10 LEM 9.4 mi(15km) above lunar surface
Dr Washington Sheffield invents the toothpaste tube
US town of Hallam, Nebraska, is wiped out by a powerful F4 tornado that sets a width record at an astounding 2.5 miles wide and also kills one local resident
Lassen Peak erupts with a powerful force, only mountain other than Mount St. Helens, to erupt in the continental US during the 20th century
Mickey Mantlehits a ball off Yankee Stadium's facade
CanadianRick Hansencompletes his 'Man in Motion' world tour in a wheelchair at Vancouver's BC Place Stadium, raising money for spinal cord research
Annika Sörenstambecomes the first woman to play on the PGA Tour in 58 years in Fort Worth, Texas
First modern sighting of the Loch Ness Monster by Aldie and John Mackay, who claim to see "something resembling a whale"
Farmers Lester Howe and Henry Wetsel discover Howe Caverns in New York state when they stumble upon a large gaping hole in the ground
Mount Nyiragongo erupts in the Democratic Republic of Congo, sending rivers of lava towards the nearby city of Goma, prompting evacuations
The SS Savannah sets sail from Savanah Harbor in Georgia on an historic journey to become the 1st steam propelled vessel to cross the Atlantic
George H. Heilmeier, American engineer (helped invent LCD displays), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 2014)
Jan Todd, American powerlifter (first woman to officially exceed 400 pounds in any powerlift - deadlift of 412 pounds 1976), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
Polish astronomerNicolaus Copernicus' remains are reburied in Frombork Cathedral, Poland after a 200 year search for his tomb
Abraham Lincolnreceives a patent (only US President to do so) for a device to lift a boat over shoals and obstructions
"Super-cali-fragil-istic-expi-ali-docious", from the film "Mary Poppin" hits #66
Yankees' future Baseball Hall of Fame 1st basemanLou Gehrigbecomes first to hit 3 HRs three times as NY outscores Philadelphia A's, 20-13; teams combine for then-record 10 homers
14th recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
HMS Beagle departs on its first voyage to survey Patagonia and Tierra del Fuego
India launches its Agni rocket
Benjamin O Davis Jr becomes 1st black major general in US Air Force
A. E. Douglassestablishes the relative dates of two archaeological sites using ancient wood samples, marking a major step forward in the dating of archaeological sites
Macedonian army ofAlexander the Greatdefeats Darius III of Persia in the Battle of the Granicus
First atlas "Theatrum Orbis Terrarum" (Theatre of the World) is published by Abraham Ortelius in Antwerp with 70 maps
Townsend Speakman 1st sells fruit-flavored carbonated drinks in Philadelphia
Toronto NBA franchise unveils name "Raptors" & logo
Tsunami generated by a magnitude 9.4-9.6 earthquake in Chile strikes Hilo, Hawaii at 01:04 AM, severely damaging virtually all coastal towns between the 37th and 44th parallels
Istanbul earthquake with estimated magnitude of 7.1, causes widespread extensive damage, followed by a significant tsunami
New deal cutting water usage of the Colorado River agreed by California, Arizona and Nevada, aims to conserve 3 million acre-feet of water by 2026
Hideaki Anno, Japanese film director and animator (Neon Genesis Evangelion), born in Ube, Yamaguchi, Japan
George Best, Northern Irish footballer (Manchester United, Northern Ireland), born in Belfast, Northern Ireland (d. 2005)
Mary Cassatt, American impressionist painter (Woman Bathing), born in Allegheny City, Pennsylvania (d. 1926)
Sun Ra [Herman Blount], American jazz pianist, composer, band leader (The Arkestra), and intergalactic messenger, born in Birmingham, Alabama (d. 1993)
First wagon train departs Independence, Missouri, for Oregon with 700 to 1,000 migrants
Leroy Buffington patents a system to build skyscrapers
Major Robert A Rushworth, USAF, takes X-15 to 30,600m
NASA launches space vehicle S-179
Train robbery at Marshfield, Indiana by the Reno Brothers Gang, who make off with $98,000
Seán Óg Ó hAilpín, Fijian-Irish hurler and Gaelic footballer (Na Piarsaigh 1994-2014), born in Rotuma, Fiji[1][2]
Pittsburgh Pirates future Baseball HOF outfielderWillie Stargellhits 3 HRs, a double and a single in a 13-6 rout of the Chicago Cubs at Wrigley Field
Riddick BoweTKOs Jesse Ferguson in 2 for heavyweight boxing title
Super Rugby Final, Canberra: ACT Brumbies claim their 2nd title with a 47-38 win over Canterbury Crusaders; Brumbies wingers Mark Gerrard (3) and Joe Roff (2) combine for 5 tries
Maggie Q [Margaret Quigley], American actress (Mission Impossible III, Nikita), born in Honolulu, Hawaii
Nazanin Boniadi, British actress and activist (The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power), born in Tehran, Iran
Charles Aznavour [Shahnour Vaghinag Aznavourian], French-Armenian singer (Monsieur Carnavel; She; Tin Drum), born in Paris, France (d. 2018)
Sean Gunn, American actor (Kirk in "Gilmore Girls"; Marvel and DC superhero films), born in St. Louis, Missouri
Randy Brown, American NBA point guard and executive (NBA C'ship 1996-98, Chicago Bulls), born in Chicago, Illinois
Shefali Shah, Indian actress (Delhi Crime), born in Bombay, New Delhi
Christian Sands, American jazz pianist and composer ("Be Water"), born in New Haven, Connecticut
Theo Hill, American jazz pianist (Intersteller Adventures), born in Albany, New York
Paul Winfield, American actor (Star Trek II, Huckleberry Finn, Mars Attack), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2004)
José Mesa, Dominican MLB baseball pitcher, 1987-2007, 2X All-Star (Cleveland Indians; Philadelphia Phillies, and 6 other teams), born in Pueblo Viejo, Dominican Republic
Jaane Niinimaa, Finnish NHL defenseman (Finland Olympic bronze 1998, Flyers), born in Raahe, Finland
Iva Davies, Australian rock guitarist and vocalist (Icehouse), born in Wauchope, New South Wales, Australia
Betty Williams, Northern Irish political activist (Nobel Peace Prize 1976), born in Belfast, Northern Ireland (d. 2020)
